channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Leveraging Azure Resource Graph Queries for Azure Redis Configuration
item.description: Scenario Many times, we receive requests for a quick and reliable way to review Azure Redis configurations such as SKU tiers, Redis versions, TLS settings, Microsoft Entra authentication status, and public network exposure. Traditionally, these checks are performed using PowerShell, Azure CLI, or...
item.dc:creator: Soma_Sekhara_Raju

item.title: Leveraging Azure Resource Graph Queries for Azure Storage Configuration
item.description: Scenario Many times, we receive requests for a quick and reliable way to check which Azure Storage features are enabled across subscriptions—such as SFTP, Hierarchical Namespace (HNS), or default access tiers. For such scenarios, customers can use PowerShell, Azure CLI, or REST APIs; however, these...
item.dc:creator: jainsourabh

item.title: How to Troubleshoot Azure Functions Host Startup Issue
item.description: Overview Azure Functions is a powerful serverless compute service that enables you to run event-driven code without managing infrastructure. When you deploy a Function App, the Azure Functions host is the runtime process responsible for discovering your functions, loading extensions and bindings,...
item.dc:creator: vikasgupta5

item.title: From Playwright Automation to Agent Driven Testing (GHCP in Action)
item.description: What Is Agent-Driven Testing? Agent-driven testing represents a revolutionary shift from traditional, hardcoded test automation to intelligent, adaptive testing powered by AI agents. Unlike conventional Playwright tests that rely on static selectors and predefined workflows, agent-driven testing...
item.dc:creator: syedarshad

item.title: Service Bus SBMP Retirement: What BizTalk Server 2020 Customers Need to Know
item.description: On September 30, 2026, the Azure Service Bus team will retire support for the Service Bus Messaging Protocol (SBMP). This is important BizTalk Server 2020 customers who use the BizTalk Service Bus (SB-Messaging) adapter, as SBMP is the protocol that adapter relies on today. To help customers...
item.dc:creator: hcamposu

item.title: GitHub Copilot meets Azure Developer CLI: AI-assisted project setup and error troubleshooting
item.description: The Azure Developer CLI (azd) now integrates with GitHub Copilot for AI-assisted project scaffolding and intelligent deployment error troubleshooting, helping you resolve common Azure errors like MissingSubscriptionRegistration, SkuNotAvailable, and StorageAccountAlreadyTaken without leaving the...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: Troubleshoot with OpenTelemetry in Azure Monitor - Public Preview
item.description: OpenTelemetry is fast becoming the industry standard for modern telemetry collection and ingestion pipelines. With Azure Monitor’s new OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) support, you can ship logs, metrics, and traces from wherever you run workloads to analyze and act on your observability data in one...
item.dc:creator: KayodePrince

item.title: Autonomous AKS Incident Response with Azure SRE Agent: From Alert to Verified Recovery in Minutes
item.description: When a Sev1 alert fires on an AKS cluster, detection is rarely the hard part. The hard part is what comes next: proving what broke, why it broke, and fixing it without widening the blast radius, all under time pressure, often at 2 a.m. Azure SRE Agent is designed to close that gap. It connects...
item.dc:creator: hailukassa

item.title: Secure, Keyless Application Access with Managed Identities - Now GA in Azure Files SMB
item.description: As enterprises modernize applications and strengthen their security posture, identity is central to how applications access shared storage. Traditional identity models relying on account keys, stored credentials, or domain‑joined infrastructure add operational overhead and introduce security risks...
item.dc:creator: Priyanka-Gangal

item.title: Announcing new hybrid deployment options for Azure Virtual Desktop
item.description: Azure Virtual Desktop is a secured, cloud-based virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) service that enables organizations to deliver Windows desktops and applications to users. Originally launched in 2019, Azure Virtual Desktop has evolved rapidly to meet the changing needs of modern workplaces,...
item.dc:creator: Steve_Downs

item.title: Troubleshooting Microsoft Foundry Accessing On‑Premises APIs Over Private Networking
item.description: Audience: Azure solution architects, network engineers, and AI practitioners deploying Microsoft Foundry in enterprise, network‑isolated environments. Scenario: Foundry Agent Service must call an on‑premises (or privately hosted) API over VPN or ExpressRoute using on‑premises corporate DNS....
item.dc:creator: pankajag

item.title: Simplify On-prem File share Migration to Azure: Discover & assess suitability using Azure Migrate
item.description: Migrating on‑premises file servers to the cloud is a complex infrastructure transformation—not just a data move. Many organizations lack the visibility needed to decide whether to rehost or modernize file shares spread across Windows and Linux servers. Azure Migrate now extends its discovery and...
item.dc:creator: bapic

item.title: Discoverable – Observable MCP Server
item.description: How to build an observable MCP Server with discoverable tools and agents. The post Discoverable – Observable MCP Server appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Colin Desmond

item.title: Building an Enterprise HR Chatbot with Multi-Strategy RAG and Live Agent Handoff on Azure
item.description: HR teams deal with thousands of employee questions every day — policy lookups, leave balances, case escalations, and sensitive topics like harassment or misconduct. AI chatbots can handle the routine stuff and free up HR advisors for the hard cases. But most chatbot projects get stuck at basic Q&A....
item.dc:creator: risthakur1213

item.title: Network Connectivity Check APIs for Logic App Standard
item.description: Introduction When your Logic App Standard is integrated with a Virtual Network (VNET), you can use these APIs to troubleshoot connectivity issues to downstream resources like SQL databases, Storage Accounts, Service Bus, Key Vault, and more. The checks run directly from the worker hosting your...
item.dc:creator: Mohammed_Barqawi

item.title: AKS App Routing's Next Chapter: Gateway API with Istio
item.description: If you've been following my previous posts on the Ingress NGINX retirement, you'll know the story so far. The community Ingress NGINX project was retired in March 2026, and Microsoft's extended support for the NGINX-based App Routing add-on runs until November 2026. I've covered migrating from...
item.dc:creator: samcogan

item.title: Step by Step Guide to Ontology and Plan for Financial Service
item.description: What We Will Build In this guide, we will construct a complete Fabric IQ solution that accomplishes the following: First, a Lakehouse that ingests publicly available data including bank financials, P2P lending statistics, borrower demographics, and licensing information. Second, a Semantic Model...
item.dc:creator: NaufalPrawironegoro

item.title: Azure Virtual Desktop in the Perth Azure Extended Zone: A Real‑World Production Deployment
item.description: Introduction Perth is one of the most geographically isolated major cities globally. For performance‑sensitive, graphics‑heavy engineering workloads, user experience can degrade significantly when desktops are hosted far from the user. For workloads such as subsurface modelling and GPU‑intensive...
item.dc:creator: akashmishra

item.title: Microsoft Fabric Operations Agent Step by Step Walkthrough
item.description: Fabric Capacity and Workspace You need a Microsoft Fabric workspace backed by a paid capacity. Trial capacities are not supported for Operations Agent. Your capacity must be provisioned in a supported region. As of April 2026, Operations Agent is available in all Microsoft Fabric regions except...
item.dc:creator: NaufalPrawironegoro

item.title: MCP Demystified: Tools vs Resources vs Prompts Explained Simply
item.description: Introduction When developers start working with Model Context Protocol (MCP), one of the most confusing parts is understanding the difference between MCP Tools, Resources, and Prompts. All three are important components in modern AI application development, but they serve completely different...
item.dc:creator: Ravindra_Kumar_Vishwakarma

item.title: Bringing AI to the Factory Floor with Foundry Local - Now in Public Preview on Azure Local
item.description: Key capabilities in this preview Foundry Local exposes standard REST and OpenAI‑compatible APIs, enabling IT and AI teams to deploy and operate local AI workloads using familiar, cloud‑aligned patterns across edge and on‑prem environments. In this public preview, we deliver the following...
item.dc:creator: liranlyabock_microsoft

item.title: ⚡Foundry Toolkit for VS Code: A Deep Dive on GA
item.description: As we shared in the announcement, Microsoft Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code is now generally available. In this deep dive, we walk through everything that’s in the GA release — from the rebrand and extension consolidation, to model experimentation, agent development, evaluations, and...
item.dc:creator: junjieli

item.title: 🏆 Agents League Winner Spotlight – Reasoning Agents Track
item.description: Agents League was designed to showcase what agentic AI can look like when developers move beyond single‑prompt interactions and start building systems that plan, reason, verify, and collaborate. Across three competitive tracks—Creative Apps, Reasoning Agents, and Enterprise Agents—participants had...
item.dc:creator: carlottacaste

item.title: New in Azure SRE Agent: Log Analytics and Application Insights Connectors
item.description: Azure SRE Agent now supports Log Analytics and Application Insights as log providers, backed by the Azure MCP Server. Connect your workspaces and App Insights resources, and the agent can query them directly during investigations. Why This Matters Log Analytics and Application Insights are common...
item.dc:creator: Dalibor_Kovacevic

item.title: Event-Driven IaC Operations with Azure SRE Agent: Terraform Drift Detection via HTTP Triggers
item.description: What Happens After terraform plan Finds Drift? If your team is like most, the answer looks something like this: A nightly terraform plan runs and finds 3 drifted resources A notification lands in Slack or Teams Someone files a ticket During the next sprint, an engineer opens 4 browser tabs —...
item.dc:creator: Vineela-Suri

item.title: Introducing the plugin marketplace for Azure API Center
item.description: Today, we're excited to announce the public preview of the plugin marketplace endpoint for Azure API Center. This new capability makes it easier than ever for developers to discover and install AI plugins — including MCP servers and skills — directly from the tools they already use, like Claude...
item.dc:creator: Sreekanth_Thirthala

item.title: Control AI spend with per-application token rate limiting using Application Network and agentgateway
item.description: Use Application Network identity and agentgateway to enforce per-application token rate limiting and control shared AI service spend on AKS.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Best of Both Worlds for Agentic Refactoring: GitHub Copilot + MicroVMs via Docker Sandbox
item.description: Legacy codebases frequently contain hardcoded logic and complex build scripts that depend on specific filesystem structures, making them notoriously difficult to modernize in isolated environments. Docker Sandbox addresses this challenge through a bidirectional workspace sync that preserves the...
item.dc:creator: cindywang

item.title: Explaining what GitHub Copilot Modernization can (and cannot do)
item.description: In the last post, we looked at the workflow: assess, plan, execute. You get reports you can review and the agent makes changes you can inspect. If you don’t know, GitHub Copilot Modernization is the new agentic tool that supports you to in modernizing older applications. Could it support you with...
item.dc:creator: PabloLopes

item.title: Introducing the Container Network Insights Agent for AKS: Now in Public Preview
item.description: We are thrilled to announce public preview of Container Network Insights Agent - Agentic AI network troubleshooting for your workloads running in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). The Challenge AKS networking is layered by design. Azure CNI, eBPF, Cilium, CoreDNS, NetworkPolicy, CiliumNetworkPolicy,...
item.dc:creator: chandanAggarwal

item.title: Microsoft Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is Now Generally Available
item.description: We are thrilled to announce that the Microsoft Foundry Toolkit for VS Code, formerly AI Toolkit, is now Generally Available (GA)! From first model prompt to production‑grade AI agents, Foundry Toolkit lets you build, debug, and ship AI end to end without ever leaving VS Code. Same Product. New...
item.dc:creator: junjieli

item.title: What’s New in Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning | April 2026
item.description: April 2026 brings three major Reinforcement Fine-Tuning updates: Global Training for o4-mini with lower per-token rates across 12+ regions, new GPT-4.1 model graders for richer reward signals, and a comprehensive RFT best practices guide to help you ship specialized models faster. The post What’s...
item.dc:creator: Blanca Li

item.title: Free Your Terminal with Detached Mode in Aspire 13.2
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings detached mode to the CLI. Run your AppHost in the background, free your terminal, and let coding agents start, inspect, and manage your app without blocking. The post Free Your Terminal with Detached Mode in Aspire 13.2 appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: James Newton-King

item.title: Claude Opus 4.7 is available on Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Anthropic continues to push the frontier of AI for real-world, production work with its new model Claude Opus 4.7. Opus 4.7 is Anthropic’s most advanced generally available model. Teams building on Azure can access Claude Opus 4.7 through Microsoft Foundry , today. This model is designed for the...
item.dc:creator: amar_badal

item.title: App attach in Azure Virtual Desktop now supports Windows Server 2025 and Windows Server 2022
item.description: Managing applications in virtualized desktop and server environments has traditionally required IT teams to bake apps directly into base images, driving image sprawl, slower updates, and higher operational overhead. App attach in Azure Virtual Desktop changes that model by enabling applications to...
item.dc:creator: Michelle_Moya

item.title: From AI to .NET: 20 VS Live! Las Vegas Sessions You Can Watch Now
item.description: In March 2026, developers came together at VS Live! Las Vegas for a full week of technical learning, hands-on exploration, and a lot of great conversations about where software development is headed next. From AI-assisted development to modern .NET, cloud-native apps, and developer productivity,...
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrer

item.title: SQL Server enabled by Azure Arc Overview
item.description: Table of Contents What is Azure Arc-enabled SQL Server? Connecting SQL Server to Azure Arc (4-step onboarding) Your SQL Server is Now in Azure (unified management) SQL Best Practices Assessment Monitoring and Governance Troubleshooting Guide Azure Arc Demo What You Can Learn from This Article This...
item.dc:creator: NaufalPrawironegoro

item.title: How Granular Delegated Admin Privileges (GDAP) allows Sentinel customers to delegate access
item.description: Simplifying Defender SIEM and XDR delegated access As Microsoft Sentinel and Defender converge into a unified experience, organizations face a fundamental challenge: the lack of a scalable, comprehensive, delegated access model that works seamlessly across Entra ID and Sentinel’s Azure Resource...
item.dc:creator: Yossi Basha

item.title: Architecting Secure and Trustworthy AI Agents with Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Co-Authored by Avneesh Kaushik Why Trust Matters for AI Agents Unlike static ML models, AI agents call tools and APIs, retrieve enterprise data, generate dynamic outputs, and can act autonomously based on their planning. This introduces expanded risk surfaces: prompt injection, data exfiltration,...
item.dc:creator: PrabhKaur

item.title: Announcing One‑Command Backup Configuration for AKS with Azure Backup
item.description: Running production workloads on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is becoming the norm for platform teams building cloud‑native applications at scale. As these environments increasingly host stateful workloads using persistent volumes, ensuring data protection and rapid recovery becomes...
item.dc:creator: shobhitgarg

item.title: Managing Multi‑Tenant Azure Resource with SRE Agent and Lighthouse
item.description: Azure SRE Agent is an AI‑powered reliability assistant that helps teams diagnose and resolve production issues faster while reducing operational toil. It analyzes logs, metrics, alerts, and deployment data to perform root cause analysis and recommend or execute mitigations with human approval. It’s...
item.dc:creator: Pranab_Mandal

item.title: Using an AI Agent to Troubleshoot and Fix Azure Function App Issues
item.description: TOC Preparation Troubleshooting Workflow Conclusion Preparation Topic: Required tools AI agent: for example, Copilot CLI / OpenCode / Hermes / OpenClaw, etc. In this example, we use Copilot CLI. Model access: for example, Anthropic Claude Opus. Relevant skills: this example does not use skills, but...
item.dc:creator: theringe

item.title: Production Cutover in Cloud-Native Migrations
item.description: Migration Planning vs Runtime Reality Migration to container orchestration platforms such as Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) typically involves: Containerizing application workloads Configuring cluster networking Migrating data to managed storage services Updating application integrations Validating...
item.dc:creator: dhruti

item.title: Databricks Delta Sharing: Enabling Cost Efficient Cross Cloud Data Access
item.description: What Is Delta Sharing? Delta sharing is an open protocol for secure data sharing that allows organizations to share live data stored in Delta Lake with external consumers—across cloud providers such as Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud—while keeping the data in its original location. Consumers access...
item.dc:creator: kirankumar_manchiwar04

item.title: Dissecting LLM Container Cold-Start: Where the Time Actually Goes
item.description: Cold-start latency determines whether GPU clusters can scale to zero, how fast they can autoscale, and whether bursty or low-QPS workloads are economically viable. Most optimization effort targets the container pull path – faster registries, lazy-pull snapshotters, different compression formats....
item.dc:creator: robcronin

item.title: Enforce Cost Limits on KQL Queries and Notebooks in the Microsoft Sentinel Data Lake
item.description: Security teams face a constant tension: run the advanced analytics you need to stay ahead of threats, or hold back to keep costs predictable. Until now, Microsoft Sentinel let you set alerts to get notified when data lake usage approached a threshold — useful for awareness, but not enough to...
item.dc:creator: shubh_khandhadia

item.title: Azure MCP tools now ship built into Visual Studio 2022 — no extension required
item.description: Azure MCP tools now ship built into Visual Studio 2022 — no extension required Azure MCP tools are now built into Visual Studio 2022 as part of the Azure development workload — no separate extension to find, install, or update. You can enable over 230 tools across 45 Azure services directly in...
item.dc:creator: Yun Jung Choi

item.title: From fragmented sites to consistent governance: Azure Arc patterns for adaptive cloud strategy.
item.description: In Manufacturing companies, hybrid architectures aren’t transitional—they’re persistent. Most large manufacturers operate across remote plants, branch sites, private datacenters, and Azure. The main challenge manufacturers face isn’t adopting cloud services, it is preventing long‑term operational...
item.dc:creator: sakshimalhotra

item.title: Gemma 4 on Azure Container Apps Serverless GPU
item.description: Every prompt you send to a hosted AI service leaves your tenant. Your code, your architecture decisions, your proprietary logic — all of it crosses a network boundary you don't control. For teams building in regulated industries or handling sensitive IP, that's not a philosophical concern. It's a...
item.dc:creator: simonjj

item.title: Stop Hunting Bugs: Meet the New Visual Studio Debugger Agent Workflow
item.description: We’ve all been there: a bug report lands in your inbox with a title like “App crashes sometimes” and zero reproduction steps. Your morning, which was supposed to be spent building new features, is now a forensic investigation. You’re setting scattershot breakpoints, staring at the call stack, and...
item.dc:creator: Harshada Hole
